description: Two dashboards report different numbers for the same metric because each redefined the calculation.
body: Failure mode: a metric like active users is implemented separately in each dashboard's SQL, and subtle differences in filters or grain make the dashboards disagree, eroding trust in the warehouse. Detection signals: stakeholders citing conflicting numbers for one metric, and a code search revealing multiple independent definitions of the same calculation. Do not reconcile by patching one dashboard to match another. Safer fallback: define the metric once in a governed metrics layer with explicit grain and filters, repoint every consumer to that definition, and verify the metric returns identical values across all consuming tools before closing the discrepancy.
